How is your career going?

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-06-21 19:27 UTC

OP

I make $28/hr as a Fire Alarm Inspection Technician. I have 2 licenses and 4.5 years of experience:
-FASA/BASA
-NICET Inspection and Testing Level 2

I deliver mail for 30 bucks an hour. Paid for 8.5 hours usually done in 5-6, it's a weird evaluated quazi salaried thing.

62384021
>>62381049
Early 40s, trapped in a flyover shithole making $80k in data analytics. Entry level title with a senior-level workload and enough experience to at least land interviews for data engineering roles with some regularity, though there are only a handful of openings in my area each year. I'm pretty frugal and on track to be able to semi-retire in Asia within a few years which is looking increasingly likely given the state of the job market and my career.

i'm a family doc and make ~130/hr. it sucks but at least my schedule is condensed into 4 days a week and i'm hoping to retire in 9 years.

>middle manager but my job is going to be outsourced in the next year or so

62388200
>>62381049
I'm 39m and a farmer. My dad retired about 10 years ago and I have taken over everything since then. Before then I had been working for him since I could walk pretty much. I remember the time before roundup ready beans and I spent most of my summers pulling weeds.

I made about $250k last year and my living expenses are about $10k. I live in town and own one of the cheapest houses in the county because my dad wouldn't let me live in a yurt in the grove. I drive a '78 F-150 I paid $400 for when I was in high school.

Anyways outside of planting and harvest I work about 3 hours per day and spend the rest of the day shitposting, checking my stocks, playing playstation 2, watching my sitcom DVD collection, and playing a round of golf at the local muni.

62388215
>>62381049
I make 60$ an hour to respond to passive aggressive emails
I got one waiting in my inbox rn.

62388275
$16 an hour at a dead end non-tech job even though I have a degree and some certs. I am the go to IT guy in my family which sometimes pays, the other day I installed a new hard drive and cleaned the pc for a family member. I didn't even ask for money since it was such a simple task but she gave me 60 bucks for it since that is what a store was going to charge her.

62388285
>>62381049
110k senior security consultant, wfh, unlimited pto, 4 years at the global company, burned out for the last 1.5 years but can't afford to quit because I got the job straight out of college through internship and I have no idea how to get another job because I refuse to do any certs or practice interviewing.

62388291
>>62388275
Why do you not have a job with your degree? Are you applying?

62388337
>>62388200
I envy the cheap pickups people can buy in farm country. on Long Island everything succumbs to rust almost immediately or is sold within the closed Spanish-speaking illegal community, so is impossible to buy.

62388396
>>62388291
Yea I've sent out over 350 applications. I've gotten my resume reviewed and edited by the alumni career services team, family members, and people in the industry. I have a home lab and document everything. I am not being picky and am willing to even take a job that pays less if it's in something tech related. Honestly it's gotten to the point where I'm close to giving up and just going to door knock for Andrew Yang so I get UBI.

62388428
39$/hour as a masonry laborer.

I started at 25$/hr in the company’s office, where I used my tech skills to build website stuff, learned and ran Google Ads and got some work for the company, which progressively rewarded me with raises.

I ran out of work at some point due to it being a small company so I was put in the field as a laborer. Now I do grunt work but get paid the same.

Although I’m not “growing”, this job feels like most feasible path for me to build my own construction company. I’ve already done jobs on the side and have my LLC open, website, SEO going on.

I want to scale my company to 10-15k/month net, but have been running into lots of cheap people when estimating jobs. It is a game of consistency, and sooner rather than later I can see my GBP with many reviews bringing me an organic pipeline of jobs, as well as eventual institutional/commercial contacts I meet here and there giving me consistent and higher margin work.

>>62381049

I don't even care about the salary or the career as long as I can be there to axe working class workers.

62389047

The reason I am ok the dole is because I have a booboo in my mental state snd alo a tiny, unused weenis

I should not have taken the job, low ambition / lazy decision on my part. I then proceeded to waste 10 years of my life with a drinking problem. I’m good now, working construction. Only physical labor could cure my fake & gay depression / alcoholism. I want to get back into IT but have not touched a computer since. What kind of IT problems are you solving on what kind of computer systems / networks? Could you give me a list or some examples of the types of problems you are solving? And any recommendations on getting back up to speed, skills, training, etc? I’m good with sales, finding business opportunities, but my IT skills have massively atrophied. Any help would be appreciated. Thanks in advance

I live with my parents so money wise I'm doing great, but in my soul I'm suffering. I hate this job, I hate working, I hate having to structure my life around it, I hate the people I meet, I hate the people I work with. Hate hate hate hate.

I've been trying to move up the food chain for a year now with no luck. At least my parents are sympathetic

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-06-21 19:50 UTC

OP

How the hell is the US still outpacing Europe for economic growth after everything that's happened?

Early replies (first 3)

62387242
>>62387239
Who cares

62387246
>>62387239
Wendeez nuts hit your chin

62387248
>>62387239
Because GDP has always been a meme.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62387318
>>62387239
Federal job cuts and govt spending in key sectors, AI efficiency gains, imperialistic tariffs, deregulation. Basic economic theory. Everything the media said was bad was actually good for GDP growth

62387322
>jump up and down on the street in front of wumbo sized Wendy, arms flailing desperately over your head, making a complete fool of yourself
>her huge eyeballs shift down to meet your diminutive form
>WENDY! WENDY! HEY WENDYYYY!
>the sky darkens as her huge smug face takes up your whole vision
>I-I-I love Wendys! I get a Breakfast Baconator every morning on the way into work! And I doordash the spicy nuggets all the time, twenty piece! W-with a Frostie, those are the best!
>she stares you into the ground for a few seconds before laughing like a thunderclap above your head
>her hand moves to one of her huge, bus sized shoes and begins slipping it off
>"well, how about a free ride for my favorite customer~?"

62387388
europe is a bureaucratic hellhole, nothing happens without a massive amount of paper work, it's a nightmare for entrepreneurs, no wonder they can't into ai, all their researchers are in america

62387389
>>62387388
/thread

You can't even shoot someone killing you in Europe. Pathetic nations all around.

62387404
>>62387239
because 2/3 of their GDP dollar value comes from AI or AI related industry, the whole shebang is just make belief and it works as long as the rest of the world agrees to play along, which it currently does. But oh boy, the second the facade drops and everyone can see the corpse they're parading around as a savior of the economy..

62387420
>>62387388
My problem (as an entrepreneur) is that to get soft funding, you have to precisely fit a mould. For example, there are lots of small check options, but only if you're associated with this university, or that university. Or only if you're a woman. Or only if you're working on climateshit. As for hard funding, investors don't want to take risk. I saw unironically a French investor who claim they only invest in pre-seed, but only if you have 100k+ ARR and their max ticket size is 250k and they exclusively lead rounds (meaning max round size is 500k). How the fuck is this pre-seed then, and why the fuck would anyone take 250k if they are already at 100k ARR? That's mostly an exception, usually the problem is they can't fucking decide and then cry about it.
But American investors are far worse, they don't want to invest in anything that's not AI. "Sorry but we only do preseed if you have clients. Oh you have clients uh... b-but did you talk to a big player ye- you did and you have a written statement that they'll buy at a high price if you can implement feature X? uh (damn, how can I say no...) oh uh w-we only invest if you have at least 100 b2b clients all paying 50k+ [these are series B stats btw]"
Worst part is Americans and their fans keep claiming it's the opposite, that American investors try to find a reason to accept while Europeans try finding a reason to reject. I want to punch Paul Graham in the face everytime he opens his lying whore mouth about America, SF investors, or YC, everything he says is the opposite of reality. But that also applies to other Americans.

62387614
>>62387239
Why wouldn't it be?

* The US has far cheaper energy and chemical feedstocks.
* The US benefits from economies of agglomeration and network effects in important growth sectors such as information tech.
* The US isn't letting itself get bossed around by the US like the EU is, which makes the US look like a better place to invest.

62387618
Is it not the most obvious answer in the world, I mean really if you actually sit down and think about it for 2 milliseconds

62387621
>>62387239
I am european and I work like 4 hours a day and I spend my days strolling, drinking with friends and playing vidya.
That's how
we're europemaxxing

62388089
>>62387239
wtf do you mean "everything that's happened"?
you mean europe fostering an underclass of people who don't and wont work? you mean europe actively building ghettoes and cultivating race riots? you mean europe taxing and regulating all innovation away? you mean europe dismantling its power plants to chase fantasies?
europe crippled itself economically and there is no financial reason to ever start a company here over the us so most of the great startups leave

62388103
>>62387621
Sounds peaceful
Good luck with the foreign invasion

62388287
>>62388089
What about the tariffs?

62388362
>>62387239
Both the US and Europe are in decline, but both have very far to fall, in spite of acceleration. The US has always outperformed Europe economically, while Europe has mogged the US in history, cultural diversity, and art.

62388366
>>62388362
And science and tech. The pipeline is often that europe invents some pivotal tech but fails to monetize it because the infrastructure for this is dogshit in europe, so either some us company copies it and wins the market, the european company moves to the us, or a us company purchases the european company and markets the tech through the main office.

62388374
>>62388366
I agree but it's also the pipeline of the past. I'd also add that the US university research system is pretty good at science and tech discovery as well. But lately, a lot of the monetization and scaling manufacturing for non software tech discovered or invented in the US or Europe has come from China.

62388389
>>62388374
Yes, it's true that modern US research unis are pretty good. However they are much better at publicizing their work than they are in actually doing novel work, in relative terms, compared to their EU peers. For example, pretty much all modern biology works on purely EU tech. But you will only read about what the US has contributed (or thinks it might contribute some day) if you look up information about biology advances.

62388394
>>62388389
I don't know as much about biology discovery pipelines admittedly, but I could see that and it makes sense.

62389689
>>62387318
> muh basic economic theory while ignoring consumer debt and wage stagnation

gdp numbers don't put food on the table for most people

62389710

europe's been coasting on welfare and red tape for decades, now they're shocked the brain drain is real.

62390217
>>62389710
Europe has been braindraining the US for a couple years now. The only reason it had gone the other way until now has been that the US is flooded with free money going around (ironic then that you blame welfare!) for business activities and salaries. But this has largely equalized for salaries (not quite there yet but you could say it's there if you add the work conditions and life conditions in the US have greatly deteriorated and many are no longer willing to get the salary difference to put up with it). It hasn't yet equalized for funding though, but it's become just hard enough to raise a business instead of a scam in the US that people who want to actually make real businesses don't feel the need to go to the US anymore (more like, US investors no longer invite them in, as it were).

Europe is just fucking stupid for regulations. Whatever you want to do you can't, and if you can it'll be fucking expensive because energy is stupidly high thanks to a bunch of globohomo regulations.

I didn't do this but I know another guy that did. I worked in a major chain pharmacy while in college and worked with a guy that would have another friend phone in prescriptions pretending to be a certain doctor that often called in to the message line and leave prescriptions on our message recorder (which is legal). Most of the prescriptions were for xanax and such, and he would ring them up himself and then sell them to pill junkies. I only found out about it when I filled in another few stores and saw the same thing there. I asked him about it and he denied it, but I knew and didn't tell anything to anyone.
Anyway he did this for a while and when there was the 10k bitcoin for pizza came out, I remember him being super bullish about it saying he worked 'extra' to buy as many bitcoin as possible. He would even take diabetic test strips and just walk out with them...outright stealing. He would show me them and say '2 boxes equals 1 bitcoin' and laugh at me. I was like wagging my finger and saying 'you'll learn'. Fast forward to 2017 and I ran into him at a gas station literally driving a Lambo. He said I should have listened to him and the last thing he said was 'Crime pays', and that was the last I heard from him.

item.title: If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for. — 57 replies — 45.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-21 18:43 UTC

OP

If you’ve held Chainlink for years, ask yourself what you’ve been waiting for.

LINK is a $5.7B token on $57.5M of annual fees. That’s ~99x revenue, and it has been flat for years, straight through every “banks are coming” cycle. The promise was always that adoption converts into value. It hasn’t. It’s been “soon” since 2020.

So here’s the question no diehard wants to answer:

What fee number, by what date, would make you sell? If LINK still earns ~$57M in 2028, is the thesis wrong, or is it still “soon”? If you can’t name the number and the date, you’re not investing in Chainlink. You’re believing in it. And belief has no exit.

Its been going on for 10 fucking years now. The jobs that had cool managers went under. Is this what it takes to run a successful comapny? Tell yoyr engineers to stop trying to do good technical work and focus on superficial appearances? If i open up my own comapny one say will I turn into what I hate? 62392906
Because I'm testing server stress load limits with API webhooks before deploying the full infrastructure on a private VPS. More users hitting the bot data feed simultaneously helps me benchmark latency.

Also, liquidity. Some microcap alerts require more volume than what my single wallet can deploy to actually trigger the breakout properly. I lose nothing by letting a few anons front-run the block data with me. Don't use it if you don't want to, it's going private in a few days anyway.

62392923
For me, its Profit Trailer with the PT Magic addon

Stop Trading Blind. Start Trading Smart.
The crypto market never sleeps — and neither should your strategy. That's where Profit Trailer and PT Magic come in.
Profit Trailer is a battle-tested automated crypto trading bot that works 24/7 on your exchange, executing buy, sell, and DCA strategies so you don't have to. It handles the grind while you live your life.
But here's where it gets powerful.
PT Magic takes Profit Trailer to a whole new level.
PT Magic analyzes price trends across your trading pairs and automatically updates your bot's settings to match current market conditions
— in real time. Forget switching manually between bear and bull settings.
While Profit Trailer on its own can switch between basic "Bear," "Bull," and "Normal" modes, PT Magic lets you define an unlimited number of nuanced market conditions
— think "Tanking," "IncomingBull," "SidewaysDown," "SuperBull," and more — each with its own tailored strategy.
What does that mean for you?
Your bot adapts automatically as the market shifts, not hours after the fact

Individual coin settings adjust based on each coin's own performance, protecting you from poorly performing assets while capitalizing on the ones running hot

A complete dashboard gives you live market conditions, a full sales history, dynamic projections, a bag analyzer, and a DCA calculator — all in one place

You can even export all buy and sell data as an Excel file, critical for anyone tracking gains for tax purposes

PT Magic is completely free
— it's a community-built addon with no additional cost on top of your Profit Trailer license
The bottom line: Profit Trailer executes your trades. PT Magic makes sure those trades are always calibrated to what the market is actually doing right now.
If you're serious about automated crypto trading, running Profit Trailer without PT Magic is leaving money on the table.
Set it up once. Let it adapt forever.

UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 105 • Updated: 2026-06-21 19:42 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

62392203
For this subject, we'll naturally be investing into and researching various different companies. Photonics is a broad field. We have to narrow down and leave no stone unturned. We'll need to find the major players in the game, the future of the industry, and how it connects with the rest of the globalized sectors.

Starting tomorrow. I also ask that you refrain from masturbating. A 90 day nofap isn't much when you're past 19, however if we all combine our psychic/wishes/dreams/aspirations/desires/vril/chi/bloodline astral roads then we can unironically combine that entire nofap and share our semen through the collective unconscious and transmute our desire.

I'll help bump from now on, I'm mostly in the background at the moment waiting for the right opportunity to onboard people.

62392239
>>62392213
Yeah eggregore anon, you did well. Honestly. Real well. Of all the first posts that could've been in my thread, I'm happy it was you. I wanted to talk about your draw idea. I have been thinking about it. I really have.

It hurts me to say this. I really am having reservations with it. I'll explain my reasoning.

It's too grand. It REALLY begs a lot from the average anon and the amount of anons who will likely frequent this general. Maybe one day this general takes off but right now it's still in the infancy stages. Let's take the photonics option as an example. To ask an anon to read an article and shill a stock here on photonics is a small ask.

To ask them to draw. Something that not even the 4chan board /ic/ is capable of doing, is a lot. That board is NOTORIOUS for the disparity of skill with its participants. If the K shaped economy was a board, that would be it.

/o/, most white men know how to fix cars, can afford a car and like fixing cars. Even if they have different quality of cars, they still all get under the hood

/diy/, while the wealth level does indeed vary, the amount of individuals who know how to plant, build, repair are all there. The subject matter for these two boards seems to repel idle minds who just nitpick other people's work. Even if you thought that mans house looks like shit, you inherently know that that man built a HOUSE

Same with /g/ where an extensive amount of people know how to program. Either due to school, work or just love for the hobby.

/ic/? All you need is a pencil or at the very least $100-$200 worth of supplies. Yet they all crab(ironic) on each other and they all know at the end of the day, it's just art. The other boards I mentioned have skills most people are forced to learn. Drawing isn't that skill.

I have one more post to say on this, give me 10 minutes before you respond

62392256
>>62374086
Seven figure net worth my life is fundamentally the same as when it was six or even five figures.

Aim for enough you're not stressing about $ don't do anything retarded and just live your life.

62392261
>>62392213
Another thing is that we want to leverage the amount of anons who will frequent these threads, and likely spend a limited time (emphasis on limited) and will only be able to make 4-5 posts before heading out.

Compare that to the amount of /ic/ anons you want to gather? A number of 10-15 draw anons is poor compared to the 50-60 unique anons who might post more on our topics of study and likely can push our plan further along

Drawing takes time and a lot of energy. We're just trying to get above $10k. If we had these /ic/ anons in here at best we'd have 4-5(AT BEST) working and 50-60 anons only able to really focus on 2/3 of the topics because one topic is gate kept for drawfags

Anon. If you want to make your own threads and own path, then go for it. I can't reasonably see it doing well here.

Finally, is the time. We've got 90 days before we switch topics. That time factor is important. We have to adapt. We can't waste a year dedicated to shrimp farming. 90 days. We have to move fast, the great reset is upon us. Drawing is antithetical to that 90 day margin.

I said what I mainly want to say. Don't feel the need to be polite. If you feel passionate about this and have to respond like a chansman then do that. I believe your plan could be done, just perhaps in another space. Not mine.

Thank you for showing me Gabriel Tenma

Desperate for answers because I've been unemployed for a few years, yeah CS meme degree if any of you are wondering. I've applied to around 400 jobs since late last year but only a few measly phone screens and no actual interviews yet. Endless rejections and I'm at the end of my rope. What type of jobs will allow me to career pivot? It seems impossible to pivot these days when every hiring manager wants 2-5 years of (proven) niche experience. That and fuck ass HR uses AI to screen candidates at literally every company these days, it's fucking impossible to get noticed in front of literally hundreds of applicants per job.

>Gematria, movies, astrology etc

Whatever helps us understand how to predict future events. I don't see why we couldn't have predicted Hantavirus, Trump's failed assassination attempt or the death of Oliver Tree.

It's time we stop showing up late to the party and screaming THEY CAN'T KEEP GETTING AWAY WITH THIS

It's time we show up early. If we can get some scripted events predicted early, we could make a bet on polymarket/kalshi or just launch a shitcoin for PENNIES and then sit back as everyone apes in.

I know this sounds farfetched but we have to believe in ourselves. If THEY can do it, so can WE.

In 2019, The most popular song was Old Town Road, Lil Nas X and Billy Ray Cyrus

Lil = Wind, Breeze, Air or Phantom (sumerian)
Nas = ???
X = death

Billy = ???
Ray = light, solar
Cyrus = Virus, Cyrus the great was said to be named after the sun

The next remix(in January 2020) had Diplo, BTS and Mason Ramsey

Mason Ramsey = this is obvious
BTS = Asia
Diplo = ???

Obvious this wouldn't be enough to predict covid-19 however it's a start. We would've known that Old Town Road was insanely popular during 2019 and we would've just had to figure out what it was alluding to. With all of us giving a shot, we might've been able to short the market.

This will be our second topic, Predictive Programming

Okay guys as we wait for the third topic, let’s go ahead and get started

First off, why should we care about photonics at this point in time? Well while the field itself might sound like sci-fi to the layman (insert schizo theories on light healing, lightsabers or consciousness transfer) we want to focus on the more grounded reality of what we’re dealing with today. AI.

We all know the Jews have massive amounts of data. They have it. How they collected is not relevant nor is the why. They have it. The main reason everyone is talking about photonics right now is because they are struggling to move the data from point A to B

Copper is what has generally handled this role, but they consume significant power, generate heat, and become less efficient at the massive scales Jews are targeting. Copper probably won’t just completely exit the industry however there will most certainly be a reduction.

Okay. Let’s stop here and take a breather. Now let us ask ourselves. What can photonics solve here, that would also make us some money?

>'hardware lottery... describes when a research idea wins because it is suited to the available software and hardware and not because the idea is superior to alternative research directions." - Sara Hooker, The Hardware Lottery, Researcher at Google
